 {"id":"bd-16n","title":"Produce golden fixtures per extension (legacy outputs)","description":"Background:
- Fixtures are the authoritative reference for conformance testing.

Steps:
- Run the capture pipeline for each extension scenario.
- Store normalized outputs as JSON fixtures in a dedicated directory (e.g., tests/conformance/fixtures/extensions).
- Include provenance metadata (extension version, capture date, legacy commit).

Acceptance:
- Every sampled extension has a fixture set covering its declared features.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:22:54.862626673Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:35:24.605609771Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-16n","depends_on_id":"bd-1oz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:35:24.605588441Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-16v","title":"Create Extension Conformance Report (evidence binder)","description":"Background:
- We need a single place that maps extension -> evidence.

Steps:
- Generate a table of extensions with version, runtime tier, features tested, and pass/fail.
- Link to fixture files and harness output logs.
- Include summary stats (coverage %, failures, gaps).

Acceptance:
- Report is self-contained and can be regenerated.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:26:16.784315729Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:40:15.941384598Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-16v","depends_on_id":"bd-16n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:40:15.941355243Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-16v","depends_on_id":"bd-31j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:40:03.153512049Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-17o","title":"RPC tests: replace MockProvider with VCR playback","description":"Goal:
- Remove MockProvider usage in tests/rpc_mode.rs and use VCR playback (real recorded streams) to validate RPC streaming behavior without mocks.

Scope:
- Replace MockProvider with a VCR-backed provider or adapter that replays recorded SSE chunks.
- Add cassette naming conventions for RPC scenarios (e.g., rpc_basic.json, rpc_toolcall.json).
- Validate real event sequences, stop reasons, and error propagation.
- Force VCR playback mode in tests (no network) and assert zero live HTTP calls.
- Emit detailed logs on each test: cassette path, expected events, actual events, and diffs.

Logging Requirements:
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) for per-test logs and auto-dump on failure.
- Log cassette path, parsed event timeline, and mismatch details (first divergent event + context).

Acceptance Criteria:
- tests/rpc_mode.rs contains no MockProvider/fake stream.
- All RPC tests run in playback mode with recorded cassettes.
- Logs include cassette name/path, parsed event timeline, and assertion diffs on failure.
- CI runs without network and still validates streaming edge cases.

Dependencies:
- Requires VCR infrastructure (bd-1pf).
- Requires recorded cassette(s) (bd-30u or RPC-specific capture).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:58:22.325926811Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:43:14.356614967Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17o","depends_on_id":"bd-1pf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:59:49.570826261Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-17o","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:58:22.335029235Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-17o","depends_on_id":"bd-30u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:59:55.584505356Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-17o","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:43:14.356590291Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1d3","title":"Implement TUI snapshot testing infrastructure","description":"# Implement TUI snapshot testing infrastructure

## Goal
Create infrastructure for testing the interactive TUI by capturing terminal output
and comparing against golden snapshots, with detailed logging.

## Background
src/interactive.rs is 2842 lines with only 2 trivial tests (<0.1% coverage).
This is the most user-visible code and needs comprehensive testing.

## Approach: Snapshot Testing

### Why Snapshots?
- TUI output is complex (ANSI codes, layout)
- Pixel-perfect testing impractical
- Snapshots capture "expected output" simply
- Easy to update when intentional changes made

### Snapshot Format
```
// tests/snapshots/tui_initial_state.snap
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Pi - AI Coding Agent                │
├─────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                     │
│ > [cursor]                          │
│                                     │
├─────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Model: claude-sonnet-4   Tokens: 0  │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) for each snapshot test.
- Log view size, theme name, state flags, and snapshot name.
- On mismatch, dump the rendered view (ANSI-stripped) + diff context.

## Determinism Requirements
- Force terminal size (80x24) and disable time-based animations/spinners in test mode.
- Strip ANSI codes for snapshots.
- Use a fixed default theme and locale.

## Implementation

### Snapshot Library
Use insta crate for Rust snapshot testing:
```toml
[dev-dependencies]
insta = { version = "1", features = ["filters"] }
```

### Test Structure
```rust
// tests/tui_snapshot.rs

use insta::assert_snapshot;
use pi::interactive::PiApp;

#[test]
fn test_initial_state() {
    let app = PiApp::new(Config::default());
    let view = app.view();
    assert_snapshot!("initial_state", strip_ansi(&view));
}
```

### ANSI Code Handling
Strip ANSI codes for deterministic snapshots:
```rust
fn strip_ansi(s: &str) -> String {
    let re = regex::Regex::new(r"\x1b\[[0-9;]*[a-zA-Z]").unwrap();
    re.replace_all(s, "").to_string()
}
```

### Width/Height Normalization
Force consistent terminal size:
```rust
fn normalized_view(app: &PiApp) -> String {
    app.view_with_size(80, 24)
}
```

## Test Scenarios

### Layout Tests
1. Initial empty state
2. Single user message
3. Single assistant message
4. Conversation with multiple messages
5. Long message with wrapping
6. Scrolled viewport

### State Tests
7. Idle state
8. Streaming text
9. Streaming thinking
10. Tool execution in progress
11. Error display
12. Slash command help

### Input Tests
13. Text in input field
14. Multi-line input
15. History navigation
16. Cursor positioning

## Dependencies
None (can start independently)

## Files
- tests/tui_snapshot.rs
- tests/snapshots/*.snap (generated)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] insta crate integrated
- [ ] ANSI stripping works
- [ ] Terminal size normalized
- [ ] 15+ snapshot tests
- [ ] Logs include snapshot metadata
- [ ] cargo insta test works
- [ ] CI validates snapshots","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:35:28.554490342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T06:19:49.018183769Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1d3","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:35:28.561095760Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1d3","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:44:35.286107381Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":17,"issue_id":"bd-1d3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress: added TUI snapshot test harness (tests/tui_snapshot.rs) using TestHarness + insta; added PiApp::set_terminal_size and PI_TEST_MODE init gating for animations; added dev-dep insta. Snapshots pending because cargo check/clippy failing due to removed reqwest/tokio deps still referenced in code; rustfmt still fails on import ordering in src/interactive.rs and provider tests.","created_at":"2026-02-03T06:19:49Z"}]}

- [ ] cargo insta test works

 {"id":"bd-1e0","title":"Implement extension discovery + install resolution","description":"Background:
- pi CLI must discover extensions from packages and CLI flags; this is core UX.

Steps:
- Extend ResourceLoader/PackageManager to resolve extension assets and manifests.
- Respect CLI flags (--extension/--no-extensions) and precedence rules (project > global).
- Produce structured diagnostics for missing/invalid/conflicting extensions.
- Ensure resolution is deterministic and cache-safe for repeatable tests.

Logging requirements:
- Diagnostics include source, resolution path, and reason codes.

Acceptance:
- Extensions can be installed, discovered, and enumerated from settings + CLI.
- Diagnostics are actionable and stable for unit/E2E assertions.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:23:58.695313308Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:40:20.054066029Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1e0","depends_on_id":"bd-gqf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:36:14.915782038Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1f5","title":"Extensions: QuickJS runtime + Pi event loop (no Node/Bun)","description":"Background:
- JS compatibility requires deterministic event loop semantics without Node/Bun.

Steps:
- Embed QuickJS via safe Rust bindings and expose a minimal Pi event loop.
- Implement microtask + promise ordering and timer scheduling.
- Map legacy APIs to connector hostcalls; propagate policy errors correctly.
- Emit structured logs for JS task scheduling and hostcall results.

Acceptance:
- A representative JS extension runs as-is with deterministic ordering.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:24:24.611922334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:56:52.824405372Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f5","depends_on_id":"bd-h04","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:24:58.543354683Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1fg","title":"Implement extension benchmarks (load, tool call, event hook)","description":"Background:
- Need measurable evidence for runtime overhead.

Steps:
- Add benchmarks for extension load/init, tool call roundtrip, event hook emission.
- Ensure benchmarks can run with a representative extension artifact.
- Record results in BENCHMARKS.md.

Acceptance:
- `cargo bench` runs extension benchmarks and outputs stable numbers.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:25:47.669514632Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:58:20.344701180Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fg","depends_on_id":"bd-1ii","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:39:01.542791111Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fg","depends_on_id":"bd-2i5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:39:10.545872664Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fg","depends_on_id":"bd-7cs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:58:20.344676604Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
